title: ricinus communis subvar. macrophyllus
intro: Ricinus communis subvar. macrophyllus, also known as the big-leafed castor oil plant, is a variety of the Ricinus communis plant that is primarily cultivated for its seeds, which are rich in ricinoleic acid. The medicinal properties of this plant lie in its seeds, which have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Ricinus communis subvar. macrophyllus include reducing inflammation and pain, treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne, and relieving symptoms of menstrual cramps and menopausal hot flashes. The herbal preparations of this plant include oil extracted from its seeds, which can be used topically or taken internally, and teas made from its leaves, which can be used to treat digestive issues and promote relaxation.
